days.title: Day 1: Forming comparatives with по-

grammar.title: Day 1: Forming comparatives with по-
grammar.explanation: To make a comparative adjective (bigger, smaller) in Bulgarian, you simply add the prefix по- to the adjective. It is always written with a hyphen and is stressed: ПО-голям (bigger), ПО-малък (smaller), ПО-висок (taller). The base adjective still agrees with the noun in gender and number, and по- attaches in front of every form: по-голям (m), по-голяма (f), по-голямо (n), по-големи (pl). Remember Bulgarian uses the present-tense verb 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са), so a full sentence is: Този град е по-голям = This city is bigger.

reading.title: Two buildings
reading.text: Тази сграда е голяма. Онази сграда е по-голяма. Моят дом е малък, а твоят дом е по-малък.
reading.translation: This building is big. That building is bigger. My home is small, and your home is smaller.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe two buildings or two homes you know. Say which one is bigger and which one is smaller using по-голям and по-малък. Make the adjective agree in gender.

days.title: Day 2: Comparing two things with от

vocab.exampleEn: The river is longer than the road.
The new car is more expensive.

grammar.title: Day 2: Comparing two things with от
grammar.explanation: To say 'X is bigger THAN Y', use по- on the adjective plus the preposition от before the second thing: Иван е по-висок от Петър = Ivan is taller than Peter. The word от means 'than' here (it also means 'from'). Because Bulgarian has no noun cases, nothing changes on the noun after от — you just put the word: по-скъп от хляба, по-дълга от реката. With pronouns, use the object form after от: от мен (than me), от теб (than you), от него (than him).

examples.english: The car is more expensive than the bread.
The river is longer than the road.

reading.title: At the shop
reading.text: — Тази кола е скъпа. — Да, но онази кола е по-скъпа от тази. — А този хляб е по-евтин от млякото.
reading.translation: — This car is expensive. — Yes, but that car is more expensive than this one. — And this bread is cheaper than the milk.

days.speakingPrompt: Compare two prices or two objects you own. Say which is more expensive or cheaper using по- ... от, for example: Моят телефон е по-скъп от твоя.

days.title: Day 3: Forming superlatives with най-

vocab.example: Това е най-голямата река в света.
vocab.exampleEn: This is the biggest river in the world.

grammar.title: Day 3: Forming superlatives with най-
grammar.explanation: The superlative ('the biggest', 'the best') is formed with the prefix най- plus the adjective, again written with a hyphen and stressed: НАЙ-голям (biggest), НАЙ-хубав (nicest). Superlatives almost always take the definite article, which is a SUFFIX on the adjective (remember: the article goes on the first adjective). So 'the biggest city' (masculine) = най-големият град, 'the biggest river' (feminine) = най-голямата река, 'the smallest child' (neuter) = най-малкото дете. To say 'in the world', say в света.

examples.russian: Това е най-голямата река в света.
examples.english: This is the biggest river in the world.

reading.title: The biggest city
reading.text: Софѝя е най-големият град в България. Това е най-старата сграда тук. Тя е много хубава.
reading.translation: Sofia is the biggest city in Bulgaria. This is the oldest building here. It is very beautiful.

days.speakingPrompt: Name the biggest city, the oldest building, or the nicest place you know. Use най- with the definite article, e.g. Това е най-хубавият град.

days.title: Day 4: Comparing adverbs and quantities (повече, по-малко)

grammar.title: Day 4: Comparing adverbs and quantities (повече, по-малко)
grammar.explanation: The same по- and най- system works with adverbs: бързо → по-бързо (faster) → най-бързо (fastest); често → по-често (more often). For 'more' and 'most' as quantities, Bulgarian uses special words: много (a lot) → повече (more) → най-много (the most). Note повече is one word (irregular), but по-малко (less/fewer) is the regular по- on малко. Use от for 'than' just like with adjectives: Той говори по-бързо от мен = He speaks faster than me.

reading.title: Speak more slowly
reading.text: — Ти говориш много бързо. Моля, говори по-бавно. — Добре. Сега имам повече време.
reading.translation: — You speak very fast. Please speak more slowly. — Okay. Now I have more time.

days.speakingPrompt: Talk about your daily habits. Say what you do more often, faster, or slower than someone else, using по-често, по-бързо, повече, по-малко.

days.title: Day 5: Saying 'as ... as' and 'the same' (толкова, същия)

vocab.example: Той е толкова висок, колкото мен.
vocab.exampleEn: He is as tall as me.

vocab.example: Тя е толкова стара, колкото него.
vocab.exampleEn: She is as old as him.

vocab.example: Той е толкова добър, колкото брат му.
vocab.exampleEn: He is as good as his brother.

grammar.title: Day 5: Saying 'as ... as' and 'the same' (толкова, същия)
grammar.explanation: Not all comparisons use по-/най-. To say two things are equal ('as tall as'), use the pair толкова + adjective + колкото: Той е толкова висок, колкото Иван = He is as tall as Ivan. To say things are 'the same', use същ (agreeing: същият/същата/същото/същите, usually with the article) or еднакъв (identical). For 'different', use различен. The verb приличам на means 'to resemble / look like': Ти приличаш на баща си = You look like your father.

examples.russian: Той е толкова висок, колкото мен.
examples.english: He is as tall as me.

reading.title: The same or different?
reading.text: — Тези два телефона еднакви ли са? — Не, различни са. Моят е по-нов, но твоят е толкова хубав, колкото моя.
reading.translation: — Are these two phones the same? — No, they are different. Mine is newer, but yours is as nice as mine.

days.speakingPrompt: Compare two people or two objects that are similar. Say in what way they are the same (толкова... колкото, същ, еднакъв) and in what way they are different (различен).

days.title: Day 6: Giving opinions and preferences

vocab.example: Мисля, че този филм е по-добър.
vocab.exampleEn: I think this film is better.

grammar.title: Day 6: Giving opinions and preferences
grammar.explanation: Now use your comparatives to express opinions. Start with мисля, че... (I think that...) or според мен (in my opinion): Мисля, че кафето е по-добро от чая. The adjective добър (good) has a slightly irregular stem in some forms but takes по-/най- normally: по-добър (better), най-добър (best); по-лош (worse), най-лош (worst). To agree, say Съгласен съм (m) / Съгласна съм (f); to prefer one thing over another, use предпочитам... пред...: Предпочитам чая пред кафето = I prefer tea over coffee.

examples.russian: Мисля, че този филм е по-добър.
examples.english: I think this film is better.

examples.russian: Според мен това е най-добрият избор.
examples.english: In my opinion this is the best choice.
I agree with you (said by a woman).

reading.title: Tea or coffee?
reading.text: — Според мен чаят е по-добър от кафето. — Не съм съгласен. Мисля, че кафето е най-хубавото нещо сутрин.
reading.translation: — In my opinion tea is better than coffee. — I don't agree. I think coffee is the nicest thing in the morning.

days.speakingPrompt: Give your opinion about two things you can compare (two cities, two foods, two films). Say which you think is better or the best, whether you agree with a friend, and which you prefer using мисля, че, предпочитам, по-добър, най-добър.

days.title: Day 7: Review — Comparing things

grammar.title: Day 7: Review — Comparatives and superlatives
grammar.explanation: This week you learned the whole comparison system. COMPARATIVE = по- + adjective/adverb (по-голям, по-бързо), with 'than' = от (по-голям от теб). SUPERLATIVE = най- + adjective/adverb, normally with the article suffix on the adjective (най-големият град, най-добрата кола). Both prefixes are hyphenated and stressed. Quantities are irregular: много → повече → най-много, but по-малко is regular. For equality use толкова... колкото, and for sameness use същ / еднакъв (different = различен). To give opinions, use мисля, че..., според мен, предпочитам... пред..., and agree with съгласен/съгласна съм. Remember: Bulgarian uses the verb 'to be' (е, са) in these sentences, and there are no noun cases — relations come from prepositions like от, в, с, на.

examples.russian: Този град е по-голям от моя.
examples.english: This city is bigger than mine.

examples.russian: Това е най-добрият ресторант в София.
examples.english: This is the best restaurant in Sofia.

examples.russian: Той е толкова висок, колкото брат му.
examples.english: He is as tall as his brother.

reading.title: Which city is better?
reading.text: — Кой град е по-хубав, София или Пловдив? — Според мен Пловдив е по-стар, но София е по-голяма. — Съгласен съм. София е най-големият град в България, но Пловдив е толкова хубав, колкото нея.
reading.translation: — Which city is nicer, Sofia or Plovdiv? — In my opinion Plovdiv is older, but Sofia is bigger. — I agree. Sofia is the biggest city in Bulgaria, but Plovdiv is as nice as it.

days.speakingPrompt: Have a short conversation comparing two cities, two restaurants, or two people. Use at least one comparative (по-), one superlative (най-), one 'than' (от), one equality phrase (толкова... колкото), and give your opinion (мисля, че / според мен) about which is best.
